content.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
content.summary:
Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well

One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.

There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to ac

content.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
content.summary:
Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent

Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.

According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximatel

content.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
content.summary:
Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped

Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull.

Heavy J was creating a poster, but not

content.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
content.summary:
Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya

Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters.

The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of

content.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
content.summary:
Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’

Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show.

The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget

content.title: ‘There’s no way they can survive’: Trump defends new 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods – US politics live
content.summary:
US president claims ‘Canada has been very tough on us over the years’ as Mark Carney says he has agreed to ‘intensify negotiations’ with US

  • Sign up for US Breaking News email

US gas prices

content.title: Spanish hotel chain ceases operations in Cuba under pressure from Washington
content.summary:
Meliá group says Donald Trump’s sanctions make it ‘impossible’ to keep any of its 34 sites open

A Spanish hotel chain is to cease all operations in Cuba at the end of the week due to “major difficulties” in doing business on the island under growing pressure from Washington.

The latest US sanctions make it “impossible, de facto and de jure, to maintain even minimal operational stability”, the Meliá group explained in a statement on Tuesday to the Spanish stock market regulator.

content.title: US claims of Cuba’s ‘unique threat’ jar with reality of hunger and blackouts
content.summary:
State department’s 100-page report is latest push in Trump administration campaign to bring down government

The US government’s latest allegations that Cuba is behind “many of the most significant upheavals in recent American political history” and that its leaders pose “a unique threat to American homeland security” have come as startling revelations to those living under the six-month-long US oil blockade in Havana, where desperate people search through piles of uncollected rubbish fo

content.title: We received no alert, say leaders of First Nations communities razed by Canadian wildfires
content.summary:
Chiefs in Ontario say they were left to self-evacuate before homes burned to ground, after being told there was no imminent threat

Fast-moving wildfires have burned two First Nations communities in Canada to the ground, as members said they were left to self-evacuate without government help or emergency alerts.

Thirteen communities in Ontario province have been ordered to evacuate because of wildfires, most of them First Nations. The Ontario premier, Doug Ford, told reporters on M

content.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
content.summary:
Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy products

Donald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced on Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products.</

content.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
content.summary: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

content.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
content.summary: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

content.title: Amid U.S.-Iran war, Houthi rebels threaten to blockade another key strait
content.summary: The Houthis, a Shia militia in Yemen that's aligned with Iran, have announced a naval blockade of a key waterway, the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

content.title: Spain's World Cup champions return home for parade in front of almost 2 million
content.summary: The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.

content.title: Powerball ticket sales begin Tuesday in the United Kingdom
content.summary: Powerball ticket sales in the United Kingdom will begin Tuesday, giving players across the Atlantic a chance at winning the same massive jackpot that U.S. players already play for.

content.title: The war in Iran has left hundreds of U.S. diplomats in limbo
content.summary: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.

content.title: The war with Iran is escalating. What next?
content.summary: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Suzanne Maloney of the Brookings Institution about the U.S. war with Iran, the increase in hostilities and what it means for the wider region

content.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
content.summary: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.

content.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
content.summary: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

content.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
content.summary: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.

content.title: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
content.summary: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

content.title: Row escalates in Ukraine over army chief's future
content.summary: Protesters are demanding Oleksandr Syrskyi be removed and former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov reinstated by Friday.

content.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
content.summary: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

content.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
content.summary: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

content.title: Dozens hurt in Bologna protests after man dies while being restrained by police
content.summary: Thousands of people took to the streets demanding justice for a Moroccan-born man who died on Sunday.

content.title: Brazil's Amazon sees sharp decline in size of area burned in 2025
content.summary: The amount of land burned in the world's largest rainforest was the smallest since MapBiomas records began in 1985.

content.title: Israeli air strike in Gaza City kills family of six, medics say
content.summary: Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children died in the attack, which the Israeli military says targeted a Hamas operative.

content.title: Shock and anger after top Ghanaian politician sentenced to 20 years for illegal mining
content.summary: Bernard Antwi Boasiako, known as "Chairman Wontumi", was also fined more than $10,000 (£7,450).

content.title: 'Anti-ICE' army veteran started fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials
content.summary: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was armed with two axes, a machete, three knives and fireworks, says the FBI.

content.title: India's 'cockroach' protest continues a day after police crackdown
content.summary: Delhi witnessed overwhelming violence on Monday, with at least 60 protesters wounded. Police said 118 of their men were also injured.

content.title: Italy’s Meloni slams protesters following clashes in Bologna
content.summary: PM pledges probe into death of Moroccan man under police restraint, condemns violence as protests injure 56 officers.

content.title: UK PM Burnham: ‘We need to be a cost-of-living government’
content.summary: At his first cabinet meeting as UK prime minister, Burnham told ministers they must be a "cost-of-living government".

content.title: FIFA ‘destroying football’ for the World Cup, says Spanish football chief
content.summary: In a scathing interview, La Liga President Javier Tebas says FIFA chief Gianni Infantino’s time is 'over'.

content.title: Israel has ‘no interest’ in joining US war on Iran, says Smotrich
content.summary: Israeli Finance Minister says Israel has "no interest" in joining the renewed US military campaign against Iran.

content.title: Saudi condemns Houthi blockade: How will the rest of the world be impacted?
content.summary: Saudi Arabia has condemned the Houthi naval blockade that could impact oil flow to importers of Saudi oil.

content.title: Searching for closure: Venezuela families refuse to give up after quakes
content.summary: Weeks after earthquakes, relatives are still searching for bodies of loved ones who disappeared beneath the rubble.

content.title: Japan’s AI gamble: Can technology offset the cost of an ageing society?
content.summary: From underground networks to emerging AI start-ups, Japan is testing whether its greatest issue can be an opportunity.

content.title: ASEAN diplomats voice ‘serious concern’ over Iran war and energy crisis
content.summary: Southeast Asian countries are highly affected by oil supply shortages caused by Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure.

content.summary: What does a heatwave cost?

content.title: 'Pentagono a corto di fondi con la guerra in Iran, Hegseth al Congresso'
content.summary: Wp, i tempi stringono per approvare nuove risorse, ma è stallo

content.title: Ex leader opposizione in Turchia sfida Erdogan, 'fondo un nuovo partito'
content.summary: Ozel, 'saremo la principale forza d'opposizione'. Media, lo seguiranno 82 deputati

content.title: As he hosts the Lebanese president, Trump says the US 'will help' the country
content.summary: US and Lebanese Presidents Donald Trump and Joseph Aoun met for the first time ever on July 20, as Trump hosted his Lebanese counterpart in the White House. This comes as the Lebanese army is taking over pilot safe zones in South Lebanon as pressure builds on the government to disarm Hezbollah and it seeks to get Israel to leave the region. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson tells us what to know from that meeting.

content.title: Replay: Lebanon's Joseph Aoun and US's Donald Trump hold press conference
content.summary: Rewatch as Lebanese President Joseph Aoun meets US President Donald Trump as pressure mounts on Beirut to disarm Hezbollah.

content.title: 'We've got to be prepared to reprioritise': UK's PM holds first cabinet meeting
content.summary: Andy Burnham told the inaugural meeting of his cabinet on July 21 that he would lead a "cost-of-living government", after unveiling an energy tax cut on his first full day as prime minister. At the same time, Burnham called for 'fiscal discipline', just as critics are doubting the VAT cut can be funded by the scrapping of former prime minister Keir Starmer's digital ID scheme. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi takes a look.

content.title: 'It has to work': France nears approval of under-15 social media ban
content.summary: France's two houses of parliament are expected to give final approval to legislation that bars under-15s from social media. The bill comes amid concern over mental health of young people. If approved, France would follow Australia's lead, where the world's first ban ​for under-16s ‌on platforms including Facebook, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube came into ⁠force in December.

content.title: Beirut hopes to gain Trump support to bring 'normality' back to south Lebanon
content.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun is set to meet with his US counterpart Donald Trump in Washington on July 21, as Beirut's military moved into an area of the south under a deal with Israel to disarm Hezbollah. The pilot 'safe zones'  comses as Lebanon is under pressure to prove that it can and will disarm Iran-backed Hezbollah, which Israel has set as a precondition for ending its occupation in southern Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis tells us more from Beirut.

content.title: India's Cockroach protests vow to continue despite police crackdown
content.summary: The leaders of India's youth-led Cockroach movement are vowing to continue protesting a day after police cracked down on demonstrators. They have been demanding the country's education minister, Dharmendra Pradhan, resign amid repeated leaks for entrance tests to medical schools. While two of the movement's members met with a government minister on July 20th, India's prime minister, Narendra Modi, is failing to address the issue for now. Delano D'Souza reports.

content.title: Microsoft strikes 'multibillion-dollar' deal with French AI firm Mistral
content.summary: Microsoft and French startup Mistral said Tuesday they had signed a "multibillion-dollar" deal for the US tech giant to use the AI developer's computing infrastructure in Europe.

content.title: Why France's D-Day landing beaches could join Unesco's heritage site list
content.summary: Normandy's D-Day landing beaches could soon be listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site -- marking the culmination of a project launched nearly 20 years ago. The designation would recognise "outstanding universal value" and raise the international profile of the beaches -- including one where Allied forces built an artificial harbour. The UN's cultural agency is set to announce its decision between July 24 and 27.

content.title: ‘I hear you’: Burnham’s pledge to ‘knock money off bills immediately’ signals politics of deliverism
content.summary: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Matthew Torbitt, former Labour Party advisor, political commentator, and anti-homelessness campaigner. With only weeks to prepare for a long-term 10-year programme for government, Burnham appears to be prioritising measures that demonstrate immediate responsiveness to economic insecurity. For Torbitt, this reflects a politics of “deliverism” rooted in Burnham’s ability to communicate empathy and urgency. His appeal rests partly on an image of ordinariness, rel

content.title: 'France wants to lead the way' with under-15s social media ban
content.summary: French lawmakers are expected to pass a bill on July 21st banning social media access to children under 15. If approved, France would follow Australia's lead, where the world's first ban ​for under-16s ‌on platforms including Facebook, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube came into ⁠force in December, as concerns grow over the impact social media has on the health and safety of minors.

content.title: China narrows AI gap, challenges US influence
content.summary: At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.

content.title: How India's youth-led 'Cockroach' movement challenges Modi
content.summary: What started as a satirical protest against exam leaks in India has grown into a wider movement voicing young Indians' anger over jobs and education.

content.title: Mexican drug lord 'El Mayo' sentenced to life without parole in US prison
content.summary: Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, a co-founder of the Sinaloa cartel and one of the world's most notorious drug traffickers, was sentenced to life in a US prison.

content.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
content.summary: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

content.title: Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine
content.summary: The strike comes as Ukraine launched more than hundreds of drones at Moscow after a Russian missile barrage on Kyiv, as President Zelenskyy sought to calm growing tensions over Ukraine’s military leadership.

content.title: UN chief Guterres to visit Cyprus in bid to revive peace talks
content.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

content.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
content.summary: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?

content.title: US ties to Israel rattle rare earth deal in Malaysia
content.summary: Malaysia's rare earth refinery in Gebeng is essential to a strategic deal between the Pentagon and Australia's Lynas mining corporation. Now, Malaysian activists are using it as leverage to support Palestinians in Gaza.

content.title: Why Japan's new imperial law keeps women off the throne
content.summary: New legislation aims to address the shrinking ranks of Japan's imperial family, while preserving male-only succession despite broad support for a female emperor.

content.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's new prime minister
content.summary: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Burnham, the former Manchester mayor, has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

content.title: Space Weather Message Code: WATA20
content.summary:
Space Weather Message Code: WATA20
Serial Number: 1116
Issue Time: 2026 Jul 19 2014 UTC

WATCH: Geomagnetic Storm Category G1 Predicted 
Highest Storm Level Predicted by Day:
Jul 20:  None (Below G1)   Jul 21:  None (Below G1)   Jul 22:  G1 (Minor)

content.title: Lunar Gateway: Earth’s guard post against asteroids?
content.summary: Humankind’s next space outpost, the lunar Gateway, will serve as a staging point to reach the surface of the Moon. A new ESA-backed study is considering whether it could also be used as a deployment point for planetary defence missions, to intercept asteroids approaching dangerously close to Earth.

content.title: ESA commissions world’s first space debris removal
content.summary: ClearSpace-1 will be the first space mission to remove an item of debris from orbit, planned for launch in 2025. The mission is being procured as a service contract with a startup-led commercial consortium, to help establish a new market for in-orbit servicing, as well as debris removal.

content.title: Hera’s radar CubeSat will peer into asteroid’s heart
content.summary: ESA’s proposed Hera asteroid mission for planetary defence will carry the smallest radar instrument ever flown in space – flying on a separate mini-spacecraft, it will approach two asteroids closer than its mothership and probe deep into their cores.

content.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
content.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.

content.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
content.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.

content.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
content.summary: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.

content.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
content.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks after KrebsOnSecurity published findings from multiple security firms connecting NetNut to the Popa botnet, a collection of at least two million devices that have been compromised by malicious software wi

content.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
content.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.

content.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
content.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].

content.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
content.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

content.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
content.summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

content.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
content.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

content.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
content.summary: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ

content.title: New York imports more electricity from Canada after high-voltage transmission line opens
content.summary: On July 3, 2026, the New York Independent System Operator (NYISO) imported 52 gigawatthours (GWh) of electricity from Canada, the most traded between the two areas since January 2025. Some of the imported electricity flowed along the new Champlain Hudson Power Express (CHPE) transmission line between Quebec, Canada, and New York City, which officially reached commercial operations in May after three years of construction.

content.title: What are tank bottoms?
content.summary: Crude oil inventories held at storage facilities in Cushing, Oklahoma, fell below 20 million barrels during the week ending June 19 until the week ending July 10, according to our Weekly Petroleum Status Report.

content.title: Petroleum markets responded to disruptions in the Middle East in the second quarter
content.summary: Petroleum markets in the second quarter of 2026 (2Q26) were characterized by continued disruptions to international crude oil and petroleum product flows through the Strait of Hormuz, contributing to higher and more volatile crude oil prices through most of the quarter. The disruptions also resulted in international buyers seeking alternative supply sources for petroleum products, driving up U.S. refinery margins, production, and exports.

content.title: Global liquefied natural gas trade volumes reached record high in 2025
content.summary: Global liquefied natural gas (LNG) trade volumes increased 5.4% to a record 56.3 billion cubic feet per day last year (Bcf/d), driven largely by U.S. LNG export capacity expanding to meet growing demand, according to a recent report from the International Group of Liquefied Natural Gas Importers (GIIGNL). Global LNG trade has slowed this year following the closure of the key export route for Qatar, the world's second-largest LNG exporter.

content.title: The United States produced more crude oil than any other country in 2025
content.summary: The United States remained the world's largest crude oil producer in 2025, according to our International Energy Statistics database, extending a streak that began in 2018 when the United States overtook Russia to become the world's leading producer.

content.title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
content.summary: U.S. petroleum exports reached a record in April as disruptions to international crude oil and refined product flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased global demand for U.S. exports. Exports increased to 13.6 million barrels per day (b/d) in April, 15% more than the previous record set in March.

content.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
content.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total e

content.title: U.S. refining capacity decreased during 2025
content.summary: U.S. operable atmospheric distillation capacity, the primary measure of refinery capacity, totaled 18.2 million barrels per calendar day (b/cd) on January 1, 2026-down over 250,000 b/cd (about 1%) compared with January 1, 2025-according to our latest annual Refinery Capacity Report.

content.title: Metered electricity demand in the New York ISO falls midday because of small-scale solar
content.summary: An increase in electricity generation from small-scale solar in New York has decreased the midday demand for metered electricity, amid overall declining load in the state. The trend is particularly notable in the early spring (March and April), when solar generation has an outsized impact because demand is relatively low and conditions for solar generation are favorable.

content.title: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June
content.summary: For the week ending June 19, 2026, U.S. refineries processed 17.1 million barrels per day (b/d) of crude oil, down 81,000 b/d from the previous week, and they operated at 96.1% capacity utilization. Gasoline production averaged 9.5 million b/d, and distillate production increased to 5.2 million b/d.

content.title: Bandi e appalti del giorno (3)
content.summary:
Per gli abbonati a QualEnergia.it PRO ogni giorno bandi e finanziamenti locali, regionali, nazionali ed europei e gare d’appalto per la fornitura di beni e servizi su energia, rinnovabili, edilizia, efficienza energetica, mobilità sostenibile. I bandi pubblicati oggi, 21 luglio 2026, su QualEnergia.it PRO: Umbria, contributi alle imprese agroindustriali per riqualificazione energetica (scadenza: 21 settembre […]

The post

content.title: Neptun Deep Production Unit Installed
content.summary: OMV Petrom completed the installation of the offshore production platform for Neptun Deep, a Black Sea project that the company says will position Romania as the European Union's biggest natural gas producer.

content.title: North America Adds 26 Rigs Week on Week
content.summary: North America went back to adding rigs week on week, according to Baker Hughes' latest North America rotary rig count.

content.title: Indian Refiners Halt Iraq Oil Loadings
content.summary: Indian state refiners typically purchase Iraqi crude under long-term contracts that requires the buyer to arrange shipping.

content.title: Norway Monthly Gas Output Up
content.summary: Norway produced nearly 12 billion cubic feet per day of natural gas in June, up 9.3 percent from the prior month and 13.4 percent compared to June 2025.

content.title: Var Energi to Acquire BlueNord
content.summary: Var Energi and BlueNord announced a merger that they said would create Europe's biggest independent oil and gas producer.

content.title: Rystad Warns 2.5MM Bpd of Saudi Oil is at Risk
content.summary: Rystad Energy warned that 2.5 million barrels per day of Saudi oil is at risk as the Houthis 'threaten to enact [a] naval blockade'.

content.title: BP to Sell Austrian Stations to Volenergy
content.summary: BP signed an agreement to divest its retail and electric vehicle charging businesses in Austria, including 250 branded retail sites, to Volenergy as part of its simplification drive.

content.title: OEUK Requests PM Visits North Sea Energy Operators
content.summary: 'Energy policy cannot be made in Westminster alone', OEUK said.

content.title: Hormuz Sees Another Ship Attack as Houthis Deepen Risks
content.summary: Another tanker was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz as renewed hostilities empty the waterway, while a threat by Houthi rebels to blockade Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea heightened regional maritime risks.

content.title: Automotive Giants Test Repsol Renewable Gasoline
content.summary: BMW and Toyota began a six-month pilot deployment of Repsol's Nexa 95 renewable gasoline to test the scalability of renewable fuel on existing vehicles.
